CheckTitle: Security group restricts MySQL access from the internet
Description: Checks that MySQL TCP port 3306 is not exposed to unrestricted IPv4 (`0.0.0.0/0`) or IPv6 (`::/0`) sources.
Risk: Public MySQL access enables credential attacks and database vulnerability exploitation, risking data disclosure, modification, and outage.

Code.CLI:
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 3306/3306 --SourceCidrIp 0.0.0.0/0
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 3306/3306 --Ipv6SourceCidrIp ::/0
Code.Other: Restrict MySQL ingress for both `SourceCidrIp` and `Ipv6SourceCidrIp` to application hosts or trusted private CIDRs.

Recommendation.Text: Do not expose TCP port 3306 to `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0`.
